mappings.rationaleAI: Angkor Wat, built by Suryavarman II in the early 12th century, its Hindu cosmic symbolism, Khmer water management, and later Buddhist transformation directly correspond to B.3.c (establishment of the Khmer state and Suryavarman II's reign) and B.3.d (Jayavarman VII).

Zheng He's Ming voyages (1405-1433) across Southeast Asia with diplomatic and tributary purposes directly correspond to B.5 (Malaya and European intrusions) and the broader Southeast Asian maritime trade context.

The Silk Road's maritime alternative routes and the spread of Buddhism and Islam through trade networks are relevant to A (South and East Asian influences on Southeast Asian civilizations) and C.2 (Srivijaya's maritime trade with China).

The Ramayana's cultural influence across South and Southeast Asia, with multiple regional versions, directly corresponds to A (Indian civilizational influence on Southeast Asian civilizations) and C.1 (introduction of Hinduism by Indian Brahmins).

Zen Buddhism's origins in Chinese Chan Buddhism and its broader East Asian transmission provide context for B.1.c (influence of Theravada Buddhism in Pagan) and the broader spread of Buddhism across Southeast Asia.
